$comment: D-CSUBSET-POINTER-DIFF-ARRAY-DECAY (C 6.5.6p9 + 6.3.2.1p3): `pointer - arrayName` / `array - pointer` / `array - array` is a pointer DIFFERENCE (ptrdiff_t = ELEMENT count) — the array operand decays to Ptr<elem> FIRST, then it is `p - q`. Frontier: sqlite FTS5 fts5TriTokenize `zOut - aBuf` (char aBuf[32]; char *zOut=aBuf) passed DIRECTLY as an int token length (no cast) -> DSS's semantic type-oracle (subtreeType/combineBinary) required BOTH operands already Ptr and never decayed an Array, so `zOut - aBuf` typed Ptr<char> -> the int context failed isAssignable -> error[S0003]. The pointer_minus_array example always CAST `(int)(t - arr)`, and the explicit pointer->int cast absorbed the mistyped Ptr — which is why it never caught this. FIX: the semantic pointer-subtraction arm decays an Array operand to Ptr<elem> (same-pointee guard, matching the HIR combineBinary c65 D-CSUBSET-POINTER-DIFF-EDGE-CASES that ALREADY lowers it via PtrToInt+Sub+SDiv). Every subtraction here is UNCAST so the SEMANTIC decay is under test; a,b (ptr-array), e (array-ptr), f (int-array, stride 4 -> element index 6 NOT 24 bytes) are load-bearing, c (ptr-ptr c40) and d (array-array) are controls. Offsets via io() keep pointers runtime. a+b = 30+12 = 42. RED-ON-DISABLE: revert the semantic pointer-sub array-decay -> `int a = p - buf;` types Ptr<char> into an int lvalue -> error[S0003], does not compile.
